title: SandboxProfile
description: A declarative policy object that defines a controlled, auditable execution environment

apiVersion.description: API version of the SandboxProfile resource (e.g. klankermaker.ai/v1alpha2)

labels.description: Arbitrary key-value labels for organization and filtering

prefix.description: Optional sandbox ID prefix. Defaults to 'sb' when omitted.

extends.description: Name of the parent profile to inherit from

email.description: Optional email signing, inbound verification, and encryption policy

signing.description: Whether outbound emails must be signed (DKIM/Ed25519)

verifyInbound.description: Whether inbound email signatures must be verified

encryption.description: Whether email content must be encrypted

alias.description: Human-friendly dot-notation name (e.g. 'research.team-a') registered in km-identities

allowedSenders.description: Patterns controlling which sandboxes may send to this sandbox: 'self', sandbox IDs, alias wildcards (build.*), or '*'

budget.description: Optional spend limits for compute and AI workloads

warningThreshold.description: Fraction of limit at which warning alerts fire (default 0.8)

artifacts.description: Optional artifact collection and S3 upload settings

paths.description: Glob patterns or directory paths to collect as artifacts

maxSizeMB.description: Maximum file size in MB to upload; 0 means unlimited

replicationRegion.description: Optional secondary AWS region to replicate artifacts to

ttl.description: Maximum sandbox lifetime as a duration string (e.g. 24h). Use '0' to disable auto-destroy.

idleTimeout.description: Duration after which an idle sandbox is torn down

teardownPolicy.description: What happens when the sandbox expires

substrate.description: Compute backend: ec2 (EC2 instance), ecs (Fargate task), or docker (local)

instanceType.description: EC2 instance type or ECS task CPU/memory size

rootVolumeSize.description: Root EBS volume size in GB. 0 or omitted uses AMI default.

additionalVolume.description: Optional extra EBS volume to attach and auto-mount (EC2 only)

mountPoint.description: Filesystem path to mount the volume at (e.g. /data)

encrypted.description: Whether the EBS volume should be encrypted at rest

additionalSnapshots.description: Snapshot-backed EBS volumes (Phase 87). Each entry materialises a fresh aws_ebs_volume from an existing EBS snapshot. EC2-only.

snapshotId.description: AWS EBS snapshot ID (8- or 17-char hex)

device.description: Optional pin to device in /dev/sd[f-p] pool

hibernation.description: Enable EC2 hibernation (on-demand only; incompatible with spot=true)

ami.description: AMI slug resolved per-region (amazon-linux-2023, ubuntu-24.04, ubuntu-22.04), or a raw AMI ID (ami-xxxxxxxx, 8-17 lowercase hex chars) for account-owned images. Raw IDs are region-scoped — must exist in spec.runtime.region. Empty or omitted defaults to amazon-linux-2023.

mountEFS.description: Mount the regional EFS shared filesystem (EC2 only, provisioned by km init)

efsMountPoint.description: Filesystem path to mount EFS at (default /shared)

vscode.description: VS Code Remote-SSH provisioning (Phase 92, replaces cli.vscodeEnabled).

enabled.description: Enable sshd + authorized_keys provisioning for VS Code Remote-SSH via SSM port-forward. Default true (omit = enabled). Set false to skip SSH provisioning.

desktop.description: KasmVNC graphical session provisioning (Phase 93). Opt-in (heavy install) — omitting or setting enabled: false skips all desktop provisioning.

enabled.description: Enable KasmVNC desktop session provisioning. Default false (opt-in; heavy install). Must be true to provision the desktop.

mode.description: Desktop mode (default kiosk). kiosk = matchbox-wm + single-browser fullscreen; full = XFCE4 full desktop.

browsers.description: Browsers to install. Subset of [firefox, chromium, chrome, brave]. Default [firefox]. For kiosk mode, browsers[0] is launched.

geometry.description: VNC display resolution in WxH format (default 1920x1080).

useBedrock.description: Route Anthropic API calls through AWS Bedrock instead of api.anthropic.com

initCommands.description: Shell commands to run after sandbox starts, before user session

initScripts.description: Local script file paths to upload to S3 and run on startup

rsync.description: Name of a saved home directory snapshot to restore on boot (created via km rsync save)

rsyncPaths.description: Paths relative to sandbox user $HOME to include in rsync snapshots. Shell wildcards supported (e.g. projects/*/config).

rsyncFileList.description: Path to a local YAML file containing additional rsync paths (resolved from operator cwd at km rsync save time).

privileged.description: Grants the sandbox user wheel group membership and passwordless sudo access. When false (default), the sandbox user has no sudo capability.

configFiles.description: Map of absolute file paths to contents, written to sandbox during bootstrap. Use to pre-seed tool config (e.g. Claude settings.json).

allowedRepos.description: List of allowed GitHub repository patterns

allowedRefs.description: List of allowed git refs (branches, tags)

allowedDNSSuffixes.description: DNS suffix patterns allowed for resolution (e.g. .amazonaws.com)

httpsOnly.description: Block plain HTTP traffic; on EC2 security groups enforce this, on Docker the proxy enforces it

enforcement.description: Network enforcement mechanism: proxy (iptables DNAT, default), ebpf (cgroup BPF, EC2-only), or both (eBPF primary + proxy for L7 inspection, EC2-only)

roleSessionDuration.description: Maximum duration for assumed role sessions

allowedRegions.description: AWS regions the sandbox may access

allowedSecretPaths.description: Allowlist of SSM Parameter Store paths the sandbox may read at boot time (Phase 89; schema declared in Phase 92). Injected as environment variables via user-data.

enabled.description: Enable Claude Code OpenTelemetry export (default: true)

logPrompts.description: Include actual prompt text in telemetry (default: false)

logToolDetails.description: Include tool parameters (bash commands, file paths) in telemetry (default: false)

tlsCapture.description: TLS/SSL plaintext capture via eBPF uprobes. Attaches to TLS library functions to capture plaintext before encryption / after decryption.

enabled.description: Enable TLS plaintext capture via uprobes

libraries.description: TLS libraries to instrument. Currently only openssl is implemented; gnutls, nss, go, rustls are schema-present but deferred.

capturePayloads.description: Capture full payload content (default: false). When false, only metadata (sizes, directions) are logged.

learnMode.description: Enable traffic observation recording on the eBPF enforcer. When true, km shell --learn can generate a SandboxProfile from observed DNS/TLS traffic.

secrets.description: Phase 89: optional SOPS-encrypted secret bundle to inject as environment variables at sandbox boot

sopsFile.description: Path (relative to profile YAML) to a SOPS-encrypted YAML bundle (.enc.yaml). Top-level keys become env vars in /etc/sandbox-secrets.env at boot.

cli.description: Operator-side defaults for km shell / km agent commands. Does not affect sandbox provisioning. Phase 92 (Wave 4) re-homed agent/claudeArgs/codexArgs into the structured spec.agent block; only noBedrock remains.

noBedrock.description: Default to --no-bedrock for km shell and km agent run (use direct Anthropic API instead of Bedrock)

agent.description: Structured agent block (Phase 92 Wave 4): default agent selection plus per-agent tool gating, trusted directories, permissions passthrough, and CLI args. Optional. Replaces cli.agent/cli.claudeArgs/cli.codexArgs.

default.description: Default agent CLI for Slack inbound dispatch and km agent run / km shell when no --claude/--codex flag is passed. Default 'claude'. Absence is equivalent to 'claude'. Phase 70.

claude.description: Claude-Code-specific tool gating, trusted directories, permissions passthrough, and CLI args.

trustedDirectories.description: Directories Claude Code trusts without prompting (settings.json trustedDirectories).

tools.description: Tool gating. Wave 5 synthesizes autoApprove into permissions.allow and deny into permissions.deny.

permissions.description: Passthrough map for Claude-Code settings.json keys not worth typing individually. The one passthrough exception (CONTEXT.md locked decision).

args.description: Extra args appended to the `claude` command when launching via km agent --claude (e.g. --dangerously-skip-permissions). User-supplied args after -- still take precedence.

codex.description: Codex-specific tool gating and CLI args.

args.description: Extra args appended to the `codex exec` command when launching via km agent run --codex (e.g. --model o4-mini). User-supplied args still take precedence.

notification.description: Operator notification policy (Phase 92): event gates + email + Slack delivery. Replaces the old cli.notify* fields. All sub-blocks are optional.

events.description: Gates which Claude Code hook events trigger a notification.

onPermission.description: Notify the operator on a Claude Notification hook (permission prompt). Default false.

onIdle.description: Notify the operator on a Claude Stop hook (idle / turn complete). Default false.

cooldownSeconds.description: Suppress notifications within N seconds of the last successful send (per-sandbox, shared across event types). Default 0 = no cooldown.

enabled.description: Enable/disable email dispatch from the notify-hook. Default behaviorally true (Phase 62 backward compat); explicit false skips email.

address.description: Override recipient for notify-hook emails. Default is the operator inbox configured in km-config.yaml.

slack.description: Slack delivery of notifications (incl. inbound chat, transcript streaming, auto-invites).

enabled.description: Enable Slack delivery for events that pass the events.* gates. Default false. Requires km slack init to have provisioned the bridge Lambda.

perSandbox.description: Create #sb-{id} channel at km create and archive at km destroy. Default false (use platform-wide shared channel). Ignored if slack.enabled is false.

channelOverride.description: Slack channel ID (must start with C) to hard-pin notifications. Mutually exclusive with perSandbox=true.

archiveOnDestroy.description: Archive the per-sandbox channel at km destroy. Default true. Only meaningful with perSandbox=true.

inbound.description: Bidirectional Slack chat (per-sandbox channel inbound, Phase 67).

enabled.description: Enable inbound dispatch. Default false. Requires slack.enabled=true and slack.perSandbox=true; incompatible with slack.channelOverride.

mentionOnly.description: Polite-bot mode (Phase 91): only process inbound messages that @-mention the bot. When omitted, the channel-mode default applies (Mode 1/3 → true, Mode 2 → false).

reactAlways.description: When true (default), the bridge posts a 👀 reaction on every dispatched message. When false, the reaction is posted ONLY on top-level engagement messages — thread replies dispatch silently. Phase 91.4/91.5. Install-level default via km-config.yaml slack.react_always.

enabled.description: Enable transcript streaming. Default false. Requires slack.enabled and slack.perSandbox; incompatible with slack.channelOverride.

invites.description: Auto-invite operators to the per-sandbox channel after km create (Phase 72).

emails.description: Emails to auto-invite. Requires slack.enabled=true. Native workspace members get conversations.invite; non-members are auto-invited via Slack Connect when useConnect is true (default).

useConnect.description: When true (default), fall back to Slack Connect for addresses that are not native workspace members. When false, external addresses are skipped with a warning.

image.description: Container image reference for this sidecar

logGroup.description: CloudWatch log group or S3 prefix
